accept: act create -- <title-starting-with-dashes> succeeds and creates the issue with the literal title (including leading dashes), or
act create <title-starting-with-dashes> fails with a clear error directing the user to use --title or some other escape mechanism.
Behavior is documented in act create --help.
payload.description: Discovered while bootstrapping act on its own v0.2 backlog (2026-05-09). Trying to create an issue with title '--description-file flag for act create and act update' fails: the parser sees -- and tries to treat the title as a flag, prints usage. Even the standard -- separator (./bin/act create -- '--title...') does not help, suggesting the custom interleaved parser added in act-65e6-followup does not honor it. Workaround was to drop the leading dashes from the title. Either honor --, or print a clear error pointing to the workaround.
payload.title: act create misparses titles starting with double-dash
